question: Describe the 'heapify' process. Given the array [4, 10, 3, 5, 1], demonstrate the steps to convert it into a max-heap by starting from the last non-leaf node and applying the heapify procedure.
expectedPoints: Identify the last non-leaf node index using floor(n/2)-1.
Compare the node with its left and right children, swapping with the larger child if necessary to maintain the max-heap property.
Complete the process for all non-leaf nodes from the last one to the root.

question: Explain why the worst-case time complexity of building a heap from an unordered array using the 'bottom-up' approach is O(n), rather than the O(n log n) that might be intuitively assumed. Justify your answer by describing how the cost of the heapify operation varies for nodes at different levels of the tree.
expectedPoints: Most nodes in a heap are in the bottom levels of the tree.
The cost of heapify is proportional to the height of the node's subtree.
The height for most nodes is small, leading to a lower per-node operation cost.
The sum of these costs converges to a linear function, O(n).

question: State the key invariant that defines a max-heap data structure.
expectedPoints: The key of any parent node is greater than or equal to the keys of its children.
This property must hold for every node in the heap.
